prompt: Topic: Some people believe that studying at university or college is the best route to a successful career, while others believe that it is better to get a job straight after school. Discuss both views.
essay: Graduating from high school is a crucial momentous transition. Therefore, the selection of whether pursuing higher education or instantly seeks a job is always a vital and hard decision for students, indeed. Some individuals supposed that enrolling in a university or college is regarded as the best route to achieve a distinguished career. To others, however, it would be beneficial for students to get a profession right after school. This essay will analyse both above views. Admittedly, there are several reasons why people believe that searching for a job straight after completing high school brings attractive merits. Firstly, high school leavers can easily find a wide range of jobs which solely require candidates to have basic conditions such as a high school degree, good health and good manner; other foundational skills directly related to the job will be trained afterwards. For instance, there are various occupations that just demand a high school degree such as jobs regarding Public Service occupy (35%) and office support occupy (40%) recruitment positions in Canada, according to Statistics Canada, Job Vacancy and Wage Survey (JVWS), (2016). Secondly, saving money is also a crucial elementary making the option of getting a job instantly after high school is a reasonable for a part of youth . For most of middle-class or lower-class families, their children are encouraged to opt for jobs straight after high school to share the burden of family expenses due to ever-growing inflation. For example, Memon Community in Karachi prefers their children to help them in expanding their family business after school, hence, seeking higher education for them is a waste of money and time. On the other hand, I would agree with the idea of pursuing higher education is more advantageous in several ways. First, possessing a bachelor allows the young gain more advantages in getting a job. Graduates, with good academic qualifications, specialized knowledge, soft skills, internship programs, cooperative work placements and many other helpful, professional curriculum and activities provided by university or college, holding vast promise for being more competitive in the job market. It is best illustrated by the following example. According to Higher Education survey which UK-based universities undertake with their recent graduates in 2017 showed that Most graduates felt that their University degree helped them to obtain their job, with 77% stating that it gave them an advantage amongst other candidates, according to a national report by the State Higher Education Executive. A further reason is that only after finishing the tertiary program, do youngsters have the chance to do postgraduate studies to achieve Doctorate, Master’s degrees and undertake high skilled employments which offer higher salary. For instance, according to a national report by the State Higher Education Exe Office Association (sheeo.org), high school graduates earn an average of almost $30,000 per year. Bachelor's graduates earn an average of just over $50,000 a year. And those with a higher level degree (master's, doctorate or professional) average nearly $70,000 per year. In conclusion, it seems evident that both choosing a job instantly after finishing school and pursuing are beneficial in their own ways. However, I am inclined toward the side that with higher academic background, students are likely to become more successful in their dream career.

prompt: Nowadays, more and more people decide to have children later in their life. What are the reasons? Do the advantages of this outweigh disadvantages?
essay: In the contemporary era, there is a common tendency for many couples to give birth at many older ages compared to the past. This trend could be explained by modernization and the development of individualism, however, I hold a belief that the disadvantages of having children later outweigh its advantages due to the harm to newborn’s health and the impact of the huge generation gap. To begin with, there are noticeable reasons why many couples tend to delay their parenthood. Firstly, the significant development of modernization leads to the tendency of youngsters to immigrate to many big cities. However, they might face a concern about the high prices in a variety of aspects such as accommodation, food or transportation. Therefore, many people usually struggle with making a living to survive in such urban areas rather than thinking about having kids. For example, in Ha Noi City - the capital of Vietnam, a couple could make around 1,000 USD per month, just enough to pay for the basic living conditions without children. Moreover, young people in the modern era tend to follow individualism, in which they totally focus on their own lives and decrease their care on other intimate relationships such as family or friendship. In many Asian countries, such as Korea or China, for instance, the young usually keep track of a model of success, which has high-paying jobs to make a great deal of money. Thus, they could splash out on penthouses or luxury cars, which enhance them to be a part of high-order societies. It is understandable that a kid would not exist in the mentioned tendency and also the least care in many young people’s lives. Despite the negative and positive impacts of this trend, I believe that its disadvantages are significantly greater than its advantages. One of the main drawbacks is that a newborn could meet many problems relating to their health. Through several years of sacrificing for their careers, the mother might not be in the springtime of youth due to the ageing process. Indeed, many reports said that the current tendency to give birth after the age of 30 could cause a number of congenital disorders such as Down Syndrome. Besides, delaying parenthood also relates to the generation gap between parents and their kids. Although kids have obeyed their moms and dads since their childhood, the juvenile could show different behaviours, especially their ideology on the way they should live the rest of their lives. For instance, there are many differences which could be seen between Gen Z and their parents, the majority are Gen Millennial and 20 years older than them. Their parents would like their children to put down roots as sustainable jobs and footholds in society in the way of hard studying for being white-collar workers. However, children nowadays would like to belong to an appropriate working environment, which improves their self-growth rather than concentrating on making money. In conclusion, the changes in modernization and individualism result in the trend of having kids at a later age than in the past. It reckons that this development could pose many problems, including infant health effects and the generation gap.

prompt: Many university graduates cannot find a job in their chosen profession. What factors may have caused this situation and what, in your opinion, should be done about it. Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay: Many students can only engage in jobs unfit for their majors after graduation, which not only significantly interferes with students’ career development, but is also a loss of the labour force. So it is necessary to figure out why this phenomenon has become universal and how to reverse this trend. First of all, universities should be blamed for this problem, in terms of its improper specialities and outdated course design, mainly caused by their unfamiliarity with current needs in job markets. Some of these universities follow the existing trend to expand their enrollment in popular majors, neglecting the saturation tendency, and as a result, oversupply is often the case. The fact that large amounts of graduates compete for quite a few positions left in their profession make most of them turn to other fields. Meanwhile, the cultivating scheme in these universities can not keep pace with the market needs, which is often insufficient in the depth of learning, in pursuit of the richness of courses. Students studying such courses are hard to meet the standards of top talents that career fairs are finding. Taking computer science as an example, programmers with a command of coding languages are all over the place, while those who have a deep understanding of algorithms and principles of computers are urgently needed personnel. Students’ lack of awareness of career planning, largely due to the environment they grew up in, can also be regarded as a reason. With the majority of decisions in these students’ lives made by their parents, students rarely think about their life goals and are thereby unable to plan what to learn. They can only gain a low professional level for lack of purposeful learning. Furthermore, they tend to focus on scores and tests rather than actual experience, as the evaluation mechanism they have experienced in their growth put too much emphasis on it, which means they merely begin to consider their jobs when the graduation is approaching, thus hurrying to land a job to make a living regardless of the professional suitability. Besides, wrong focus also brings them less capability in social contact and other essential practical abilities required in the workplace, while the less competitiveness can only lead them to be replaceable, reducing the likelihood of gaining the scarce position in their majors. Both market and universities can take measures to tackle the problems. For the companies in the talent markets, they can regularly invite professors or university administrators to forums or conferences that share the latest development in the industry and demands for employees, in order to narrow the gap between the academy and working world, as well as provide universities with adequate information to guide their course structure improvement. In the meantime, universities can hold more activities related to occupational planning such as lectures from successful businessmen and visits to large enterprises. It is also advisable to add more internships to the credit requirement, to push students to be exposed to the workplace as early as possible. In conclusion, universities’ curriculum and students’ lack of career-planning awareness contribute to graduates’ difficulty in seeking professional-appropriate jobs. Companies and educational institutions’ actions can help address the issue.

prompt: Write about the following topic.Some people think that art is an essential subject for children at school while others think it is a waste of time.Discuss both views and give your opinionGive reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
The education of children is surely one of the hottest topic debated. What are the subjects and the skills that children have to learn and what help them to grow up better? According to one view, art is vital for the growht of children and therefore it has to be a school subject. On the other hand, there are people who think that to do art at school is a waste of time. I believe that to study art is fundamental for at least two different factors and then it should be teached in the schools. Surely, more dignity should be deserved to it.
 To study art at the different level of education is essential for the development of certain skills. First of all, those children who play with art develop the ability to measure, to structure a picture as well as delineate the border of a page earlier than those children who are not educated in drawing. They start recognising pictures and to distinguish between the colors and their possible mixtures. The second reason, by contrast, is linked with some psychological implications that to do art involves. To do art does not mean just to draw. Art can help adults to understand children and their needs. Children, thanks to the drawing activity, can start expressing their emotions and desire using pictures and colors. They can start working with their imagination before they learn the acquire the writing and reading skills. These two reasons lead some people to believe that is fundamental for children to do art. Then, art has to be a subject and considered as useful as Mathematics and English. The development of creativity and critical thinking that art implies is extraordinary useful. Indeed, children are led to understand practically what they have to do if they commit an error when they are drawing. They start to think about the errors they can made as possibility to do something new and original.
 By contrast, there are people who dislike the idea that art can be compared to other subjects like Math and Science. They believe that art is more an hobby than something useful. Since there are no notions that a child can memorise and there are not test a teacher can prepare to understand the level of the children, art cannot be considered a subject. Therefore, there are no reasons to teach it in schools. However, the reasons presented by those who do not want to add art in the list of school subjects are misguided. They start from the idea that to learn means to stay hours in front of a book. This is obsiusly true but it is not enough. There is a fundamental part of learning that is advantaged by practical activities like art.
 In conclusion, I believe that art is a subject and should be considered as such. The notions learnt by drawing are as essential as the first basis of grammar, keeping the necessary differences. Since children have to develop different skills, art as a school subject should be supported because it help children to develop at least creativity and critical reasoning as well as to express their emotions.

prompt: Some people believe that studying at university or college is the best route to a successful career, while others believe that it is better to get a job straight after school. Discuss both views and give your opinion?
essay: Many believe that pursuing a higher education through university is the best way to have a successful career. However, others think that getting a job right after school is better. With the rising standards of living, people want to earn money quickly to satisfy their needs, all while being successful. So, which of these two paths is able to satisfy people's demands? Higher education is the first step towards a better future. Although there are some exceptions that defy this rule like Bill Gates, for instance. It still holds true for the vast majority. College studies enrich your mind and enable you to discover knowledge that you would not uncover alone. Not to mention that there are professionals who can aid you with your studies and career. Furthermore, you do not just gain academic experience you gain so much more, as you get involved in the university life; getting to know people of different backgrounds, nationalities and cultures will open up your mind and give you better adaptation and socializing skills. Whatever you learn or experience during college will aid you in your professional life. It also makes you an expert on what you do, there is a difference between someone who just states their opinion on a matter and between someone who actually have information about that matter. Nevertheless, some people don't believe much in higher studies. They just want to get a job after they graduate high school and start building a future for themselves. These people tend to invest the years that would be spent in a certain faculty in pursuing a career. Some of these people have their own skills and talents that will allow them to build a future on their own. For example, some artists would start selling their arts at a young age and wouldn't study art, they'd open an art gallery and create their own business.The same could be said about actors and singers, of course they would need to train to improve their talents, but they can still manage without pursuing a higher studies. Others just prefer working than studying. In my opinion, I believe that both sets of paths are right. As much as I believe that studying at university does make a difference and adds to your skills and knowledge, it's not a rule. Some of the greatest leaders in the world didn't get a chance at a higher education, take Mohamed Ali, for instance, he built a great empire, improved Egypt's educational and economic system, all while being illiterate. Now, I am not encouraging illiteracy but I believe hat everyone should do what is best for them. Set a goal and do what it takes to achieve that goal. If you want to be a doctor, you'd have to go to medical school. Thus, you must determine what you want and take the right decisions to attain your desires. In conclusion, it's our goals and dreams that determine what sort of action we'd take. However, one must know that success is a matter of determination and hard work, whether they choose to go to college or open up their own business.

prompt: In many countries, the age at which people can retire is being raised. Some people argue that this is a necessary measure to ensure the financial stability of the pension system, while others believe that it puts an excessive burden on the aging population. Discuss both these views and give your own opinion. Provide relevant examples and reasons to support your answer.
essay:
Many doctors are concerned about the high use of computer games by children and young people. What mental and physical problems may arise from excessive use of these games? How could these problems be reduced?

Although using games in early ages or even in elderly have some benefits for keep you motivate and help people to be creative, In recent years, with developing the technology and gaming industry, scientific research shows a significant effect of online and offline games on young generation health by overusing of these games, such as their mental and physical conditions. 

Given that with developing the Technology, the game and entertainment industry had a amazing change by time, and looking for more customers, the young generation are important target for using and testing these games. So, it is predictable that the most long-term effects of using these games include teenagers as well. Since most of these games probably are online and needs to sit and using a computer or  a smart television, it could be expected children experience  some desise and health issues, such as obesity, eye problem, anxiety and probably being unsocialize by time. For instance, maybe they will tend to eat more fast food, spend more time in home without any activity or exercise, being by their own in their room, instead of doing outdoor activity with their parents and friends. Moreover, some of these games may show violence, which have a strong effect on the mental health and make the teenagers more anxious undoubtedly, leading them to the depression in long_term. 

However, despite some advantage which could be consider for gaming during some certain ages, for example, raisins creativity in kids under 8, or even keep the brain activity in elderly, maybe the entertainment industry needs some regulation and strict rules for games content and the range of ages that suppose to use these games. In addition, parental controls on children activity could be another solution in some cases, while they try to spend more time with their teenagers on outdoor activity. Also, inform  young generation about the  backwards of some games which shows huge amount of violence science and how these games could influence their mental health, would be another useful solution. In addition, in my opinion government and mayers can establish some facilities and game centers for families and young people to experience some of these interesting games out of the screen, for instance, paint_ball centers or some activities like these, which help people to have fun and experience raising adrenalin at the same time. 

In conclusion, although gaming have uncountable advantage in many cases, albeit ther is no doubt being busy with computer games in long term and with hight hour sitting front of screen, specially in children and adolescents, huge amount of physical and mental issues and problems are not unexpected. Authorizing the rules for entertainment industry and build some game center in one hand, and more parental control on children activity and try to be attend in outdoor activity with them, on the other hand may be the example of some good solution for having more healthy future generations.

prompt: The only way to improve road safety is to give much stricter punishments on driving offenses. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay: In modern society, education plays a vital role in human life. The first step in education is a school. It has become clear in recent years that the dress code in the schools is one of the issues that people feel strong about it. Some people claim that kids during their education should wear specific clothes to show equality among pupils, while others believe that every child should be a person which will help them in the future to be creative and innovative. In my opinion, the dress code is a good idea to focus more on education than appearance. Many consider that each person is unique and to save their individuality, they should have a choice of what to wear. For example, girls spend a lot of time in the morning by selecting clothes which they will wear for the lessons and by this they can develop a decision-making process which will help them in the future to be a person. Another point is that from the early years' humans will value democratic views which has a lot of good points, for instance, the child with such values will not practice the dictator regime in the future. Moreover avoiding dress code in the learning institutions for girls and boys has a connection with equality. It is widely believed that a teacher could wear clothes that she or he wants and in this case, a student also should have such an opportunity. On the other hand, giving the right to wear whatever the pupil wants in the school not always refer the equality. It is certainly true that the financial situation of children's families could be different. Kids from poor families could have a big chance to be discriminated against just because of the appearance, while pupils from reach families can learn a value by having expensive clothes. For example one of the research showed that kids without modern clothes bullied more, and this is one of the aspects which can destroy the psyche of people. That’s why it is important to have a uniform, which is the main factor of reducing conflicts in the schools. Some people suppose that If schools will practice free uniform, then the child will focus more on social aspects of life, which will interpret them from education. Discussing fashion, brands will take a lot of time, which can be used for a study. Children should focus attention more on his or her subjects and not on appearance because real knowledge can develop individuality of the person, rather than the clothing. For example student with a high IQ and good knowledge could be successful in the future, because critical thinking will be developed and these will lead to innovative ideas. Parents should be sure that the main purpose of their instant for going to school is to get an education and not showing themselves to others. Taking all these points into consideration, equality and good education depends on a dress code. Uniforms can stop conflicts and discrimination. In my opinion, free dress code can develop unique individuality but arguments for wearing uniform is more important.

prompt: In many countries people increasingly talk about money (how much they earn or how much they pay for things in their daily conversations). Why? Is this a positive or negative development?
essay:
In modern society, most people believe that money is as important and indispensable as water. As a result, in many countries, people talk more and more about money, such as how much they earn or pay for things in everyday conversation).
 This phenomenon, I think is a negative development. And I have three reasons for this:
 1. Inner hypocrisy;
 2. Lack of confidence in oneself;
 3. Thinking and vision.
 First of all, the inner hypocrisy. Many young people like to talk to their friends about how much they earn and how many expensive things they buy. At the college reunion, everyone should introduce himself or herself. For example, what kind of work he or she is doing now; Recently bought a house and a car somewhere, and then has to recommend it to others. The core purpose is to say "I've made millions in just a few years and bought a house and a car." Although they look nice on the outside, they are empty inside. Therefore, the inner hypocrisy is because they are empty in their heart and have nothing else to compare with others. They can use the money to make up for and satisfy their inner emptiness, and only in others can they find their sense of existence, recognition, and attention to themselves.
 Secondly, I am not confident in myself. Many elderly people like to chat about their children while playing cards under the community pavilion. For example: if your child is married; How much does your child earn in a month; Did your child buy you anything expensive? If you answer in the negative, you will be looked down upon, even saying, "Your children are working such long hours for so little money and they don't buy you anything nice." These people often talk about it because they are not confident in themselves and they know that their children are inferior in abilities and other aspects. In other words, they are uncertain and reckless. So they like to show off in front of each other and want to prove themselves and their ability to each other.
 Finally, the thinking and vision are not high. Every country or place has different classes of people, such as those living in rural China and; Paternal (they all lived in the past); on the surface look good in every way, but only after material things. Because they think money is the most important, money can do a lot of things, and only money can give them a sense of security. As can be seen from this, a person's circle of friends and living environment can determine a person's thinking and vision, so, only if your surroundings are positive, can attract more positive people like you.
 In a word, if a person always talks about money, it shows that he is hypocritical inside, not confident in himself, thinking and vision are not high. So. Be sure to constantly improve your thinking and vision, constantly improve your ability in all aspects, and even keep positive at all times, so that not only can attract more people like you, but also your future life will be better.

prompt: Nowadays, more and more people decide to have children later in their life. What do you think are the reasons for this? Do the advantages of the trend outweigh the disadvantages?
essay: In recent decades, more and more individuals decide to delay their parenthood. Statistics show that the average age for parenthood rose to 30, nine years older than it was in 1970. The major reasons behind this trend are the increasing living costs, social changes, and personal hobbies. Even though a child later in life can lead to increased health risk for the woman and the baby, older parents are more financially stable and emotionally prepared which allows their children to properly grow physically and mentally. Living prices have risen dramatically over the years, and having a kid is highly expensive, causing young couples to be hesitant. A child comes with an infinite number of additional costs, including medical, educational, and fundamental human requirements. These are costs that many young people cannot afford. Having a child necessitates a significant amount of personal time as well. A parent must devote time to caring for their infant, feeding them and cleaning up after them. A lot of young couples might not have the time required now that society has changed. Unlike society back in the early 20th century where people became parents at a much earlier age, every person now has to go to work, get higher education, and many other duties. Not only do young people need to fulfill their duties, they also need to satisfy their personal needs, such as traveling the world, focusing on their education, and working towards their career to improve their financial status. These are the main factors that encourage people to become parents at a later age in life. Having children at an older age comes with both disadvantages and advantages. Giving birth at an older age increases the chance of miscarriage, which is harmful for the woman’s health afterwards. According to doctors, among women who are 35 or older, the rate of stillbirth is 5.8 per 1000 newborns. The baby also has a chance of a lighter birth weight, more abnormalities associated with chromosomes, and other negative outcomes. Despite the health concerns, later parental age comes with many advantages. It is proven that older parents are more economically free and educated. The reason being they had the chance to focus on their education and careers when they were young without having a child to hinder them from those activities. Being more financially stable and emotionally prepared are fundamental to the development of their child. For instance, with more economic freedom, the parents can be able to afford healthy foods and good medical products which ensures the child’s physical growth and health. They can also give their kids more educational experiences at a young age, such as letting them travel to different places in the world and giving them access to good schooling. This allows the child to develop mentally and intelligently and unlock more opportunities in their future. In essence, ​the main causes behind delayed parenthood are rising living costs, social changes, and personal interests. Even while having a child later in life increases the woman's and the baby's health risks, older parents are more financially stable and emotionally prepared, allowing their children to thrive physically and psychologically.

prompt: Some believe the government should take care of retirees, while others think everyone should save for their own retirement (opinion)
essay: Some people believe that the government should take care of old people and provide financial support after they retire. Others say individuals should save during their working years to fund their own retirement. What is your opinion? Give reasons for your answer and include examples from your own experience.It is true that when people advance into their golden years, they require more help and attention. For that reason, a significant number of people hold the opinion that the government should provide financial assistance to the elderly. However, I tend to agree with those who argue that workers should be saving a portion of their income to have a nest egg in their retirement.To begin with, a culture of saving rather than depending on the government may foster the overall economic development of a country. In other words, if people were to accumulate their own savings for their pension age, it would lessen the financial burden on the government. As a result, the government would be able to release more funds to more productive purposes, such as development of infrastructure, industries, hospitals, and so on, which may expedite the overall progression of a country.In addition to economic factors, putting money aside has some personal benefits. Since savings-oriented people are not dependent on the state for their living expenses, this may instill a sense of self-respect and pride in them. On the other hand, those who are reliant on financial assistance very often face the negative societal stigma, as many people consider them to be inferior and unworthy members of society. Hence, to live a life with dignity and pride, taking responsibility for funding oneâ€™s own retirement is evidently a better option from individual perspective.In contrast, the provision of monetary outlays for retirees has proven unfeasible in many countries. Since in many nations the number of senior citizens is on the rise, in the long run governments are likely to struggle to sustain the cost of providing for the elderly. The early signs of this have been seen in many countries that were forced to raise the retirement age, including my home country where men can now retire at the age of 67 instead of 65. Therefore, in the foreseeable future, these kinds of support may become completely unsustainable.In conclusion, the provision of state monetary support may undoubtedly lessen the concern of retirees to some extent. However, considering the issue of sustainability of this provision, it is better to opt for individual savings from the very beginning of working life, for the benefit of both individual and national development.This response addresses all parts of the topic, by discussing the merits of funding oneâ€™s own retirement versus relying on government pension. The writerâ€™s opinion has been stated as well and is very clear. The neat presentation of ideas has been helped by appropriate paragraphing, and cohesion and coherence are maintained throughout the essay. The authorâ€™s vocabulary seems to be rich and there is a variety of sentence structures used in this essay. Overall, this essay deserves an IELTS Band 8.

prompt: Write about the following topic.Due to the development and rapid expansion of supermarket in some countries, many small, local business are unable to compete. Some people think that the closure of local business will bring about the death of local communities.To what extent do you agree or disagree?Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
Due to globalisation the expanding of international brands and marketing is caughting on over years. This phaenomenon have caused the development and expansion of supermaket but also the closure of local businesses. I agree with the part of people who think that local business play a vital role in the life of local commuities that will be almost death in the next few years.
 In Italy 50 years ago to buy food people had to go to street market. They used to buy km 0 products respecting the season of production of fruit of vegetables. People have to talk to each other, people have to go shopping respecting the workhours of people who had to produce the products and then sold them. In the last year, supermaket have caught on. Their goal is to offer how many products possible, without taking care of the season in which a certain product should be found in the market. For instace, if you want to buy cherries in winter now you can to to the nearest supermarket and find them, even if is November. By contrast, before supermarket's developement people had to wait the productive season of cherries and waint until summer to eat them. This phaenomenon had take people to prefer supermarket because they can find more products. However the consequences of this choice are in my opinion dramatic.
 First of all, the clousure of local business is causing a breakdown on the economical status of different countries. For instance, in Sicily there are still different people who work the ground and survive because they produce and sell their own products. It used to be their job. However, because of the supermakert their job is not apprecciated and they are losing money and the possibility to continue their job. This dramatic phaenomenon is surely causing the death of local communities because a vital part of the community itself is losing job. Another example coming from my personal experience might be the following. Nowadays you can buy a product to clean and polish your shoes in a supermaket. 50 years ago my grandfather used to go to the city center where a man used to clean and polish the shoes for him. It was his job. I had the extraordinary opportunity to meet this old man who clean and polish my shoes for little money. He was happy because, even if he is conscious that is almost impossibile, he hope to have the opportunity to do this job as long as possible. He told me that to clean shoes on the street for fifty years have given him the opportunity to meet people and to talk with them. After I have known him, I thought that because of globalisation we are losing our roots as well as the possibility of knowing our own place and people.
 In conclusion, I believe that the phaenomenon of supermarket's expansion is causing the death of local communities. I also think that, although time changes and new habits are followed, we have at least to be conscious and recognise what we are losing.
